zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Dacre於大約1433年出生在英國諾圖波藍德吉爾曼德時，她的父親Sir Thomas Dacre25歲，母親Elizabeth Bowett29歲。她於1446年6月之後與Sir Richard Fiennes在英國倫敦結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1485年3月8日在英國素可賀斯特蒙瑟享年53歲，埋葬在英國素可賀斯特蒙瑟。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Dacre于大约1433年出生在英国諾圖波藍德吉爾曼德时，她的父亲Sir Thomas Dacre25岁，母亲Elizabeth Bowett29岁。她于1446年6月之后与Sir Richard Fiennes在英国倫敦结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1485年3月8日在英国素可賀斯特蒙瑟享年53岁，埋葬在英国素可賀斯特蒙瑟。

nameMeanings.name: Dacre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from either of two places called Dacre, one in Cumbria, the other in Yorkshire.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Dacre was born about 1433, in Gilsland, Northumberland, England, her father, Sir Thomas Dacre, was 25 and her mother, Elizabeth Bowett, was 29. She married Sir Richard Fiennes after June 1446, in London, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 8 March 1485, in Herstmonceux, Sussex, England, at the age of 53, and was buried in Herstmonceux, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Dacre est née environ 1433, à Gilsland, Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, Sir Thomas Dacre, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Bowett, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Richard Fiennes après juin 1446, à Londres, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 8 mars 1485, à Herstmonceux, Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterrée à Herstmonceux, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Dacre um 1433 in Gilsland, Northumberland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Thomas Dacre 25 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Bowett 29. Sie hat nach Juni 1446 in London, England Sir Richard Fiennes geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 8. März 1485 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Herstmonceux, Sussex, England und wurde in Herstmonceux, Sussex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Dacre è nata il circa 1433 nel Gilsland, Northumberland, England, sua padre, Sir Thomas Dacre, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Bowett, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Sir Richard Fiennes dopo il giugno 1446, nel London, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 8 marzo 1485, nel Herstmonceux, Sussex, England, all'età di 53 anni, e fu sepolta a Herstmonceux, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Dacreが約1433年にイギリスノースウンバーランドギルスランドで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas Dacreは25歳，母Elizabeth Bowettは29歳であった。1446年6月以後にイギリスロンドンでSir Richard Fiennesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1485年3月8日にイギリススセックスハーストモンセウックスで53歳で死去し，イギリススセックスハーストモンセウックスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Dacre(이)가 대략 1433년 영국 노르스벌랜드 길슬랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Dacre(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Bowett(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1446년 6월 이후 영국 런던에서 Sir Richard Fiennes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1485년 3월 8일 영국 수세스 허스트몬스우스에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 허스트몬스우스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Dacre nasceu aproximadamente 1433, em Gilsland, Northumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Thomas Dacre, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Bowett, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Richard Fiennes após junho de 1446, em London, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 8 de março de 1485, em Herstmonceux, Sussex, Inglaterra, com 53 anos, e foi sepultada em Herstmonceux, Sussex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Dacre родилась около 1433 г. в местности джилсланд, нортумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Thomas Dacre было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Bowett, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard Fiennes после июнь 1446 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 8 марта 1485 г. в местности хирстмонсеукс, суссекс, Англия в возрасте 53 лет и была похоронена в местности хирстмонсеукс, суссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Dacre nació aproximadamente 1433, en Gilsland, Northumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Thomas Dacre, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Bowett, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Sir Richard Fiennes después de junio de 1446, en London, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 8 de marzo de 1485, en Herstmonceux, Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultada en Herstmonceux, Sussex, Inglaterra.
